requested.title_after: [Bug]: docs Packages nav link points to /packages instead of opening packages.meshlang.dev in a new tab
requested.body_after:
## Before you submit
- [x] I searched existing issues and checked the relevant docs/examples first.
- [x] I can reproduce this with a current build, the latest public release, or a specific commit I list below.

## Affected area
documentation / website

## Version / commit
79a030c8

## Operating system
Other

## Project / starter / path (if relevant)
- `website/docs/.vitepress/config.mts`
- `website/docs/.vitepress/theme/components/NavBar.vue`

## Reproduction steps
1. Open the website.
2. Click the **Packages** link in the top navigation bar.
3. Observe that it navigates the current tab to `/packages`.

## Expected behavior
The **Packages** nav item should open `https://packages.meshlang.dev` in a new browser tab.

## Actual behavior
The nav item points at the local site route (`/packages` / `/packages/`) instead of the external packages site, so it keeps the user on the docs site and sends them to the wrong destination.

## Logs, errors, or screenshots
Relevant repo surfaces currently point to the local path:
- `website/docs/.vitepress/config.mts:218` → `{ text: 'Packages', link: '/packages/' }`
- `website/docs/.vitepress/theme/components/NavBar.vue:17` → `{ text: 'Packages', href: '/packages/' }`

## Additional context
This appears to affect the shared website navigation surface rather than page-local content. The desired behavior is an external link to `packages.meshlang.dev`, opened in a new tab/window.
requested.comment_body: This issue transfers to `hyperpush-org/mesh-lang` so the existing bug history stays attached to the docs surface that actually owns the code.

requested.title_after: [Feature]: record shipped /pitch evaluator route explicitly
requested.body_after:
## Area
landing app

## Problem statement
The evaluator-facing `/pitch` route already shipped in the product repo, but there is still no dedicated Hyperpush issue that records that surface directly.

## Proposed solution
Create one retrospective product-repo issue for the shipped `/pitch` surface, point it at `mesher/landing/app/pitch/page.tsx`, and then close it as completed so repo issue history matches code truth.

## Alternatives considered
Leaving `/pitch` implicit in milestone history keeps repo drift hidden, and repurposing an unrelated issue would destroy the audit trail for the shipped route.

## Expected impact
S02 and S03 get an explicit canonical issue URL for `/pitch`, and maintainers can trust that the product repo issue list covers shipped evaluator-facing surfaces.

## Additional context
- Code surface: `mesher/landing/app/pitch/page.tsx`
- Shipped proof: `.gsd/milestones/M056/M056-SUMMARY.md`
- This is a retrospective repo-truth issue, not new implementation work.
requested.comment_body:
Closing as completed retroactively. The `/pitch` evaluator surface already shipped during M056, and this issue exists to make the repo issue history truthful and explicit.

Evidence: `mesher/landing/app/pitch/page.tsx`, `.gsd/milestones/M056/M056-SUMMARY.md`.

requested.title_after: Mesh: clustered Docker-on-VM deploy baseline for Hyperpush
requested.body_after:
## Outcome
Give Hyperpush a truthful, repeatable Docker-first deployment baseline on a generic VM with Mesh clustering that works without repo-local magic.

## Scope
- Generic Docker-on-a-VM deployment path for Mesh clustered apps
- Node identity, discovery, clustering, and bootstrap UX must be stable enough for Hyperpush
- Container/runtime contract should be simple enough to document and automate
- Close the gap between "language supports clustering" and "operator can actually deploy this product"

## Acceptance criteria
- A generic VM + Docker path exists and is documented
- The clustered runtime starts, discovers peers, and stays healthy under the Hyperpush shape
- The deploy path is truthful for both single-node and clustered product deployments
- Any required env/entrypoint/runtime assumptions are explicit and tested

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Mesh
- Track: Mesh Foundation
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 1

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Generic Docker-on-a-VM cluster bootstrap is documented and repeatable.
- Peer discovery, identity, and health verification work without repo-local magic.
- Single-node and clustered product deployments are both truthful.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/7 — Mesh: define generic Docker cluster bootstrap contract
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/8 — Mesh: ship reference clustered VM deployment and health verification
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

Closing as shipped. The S01 reconciliation ledger marked this row as stale shipped-but-open scope, and the delivered work is already covered by the M053/M054 Mesh launch foundations proof.

Evidence: `.gsd/milestones/M053/M053-SUMMARY.md`, `.gsd/milestones/M054/M054-SUMMARY.md`.

requested.title_after: Mesh: runtime hardening for Hyperpush production load and clustering
requested.body_after:
## Outcome
Harden the Mesh runtime so Hyperpush can honestly claim production readiness under real clustered load, failover, and recovery conditions.

## Scope
- Cluster stability under Hyperpush production traffic patterns
- Failover, recovery, and continuity behavior required by the product
- Runtime diagnostics and observability needed to debug real incidents
- Any correctness or performance issues that would make Hyperpush's core promises fake

## Acceptance criteria
- Runtime behavior is stable enough for Hyperpush production use
- Known crashers, correctness bugs, or continuity gaps on the Hyperpush path are closed or explicitly removed from public claims
- Cluster health, recovery, and failure surfaces are inspectable by operators

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Mesh
- Track: Mesh Foundation
- Commitment: Committed
- Critical path: 1

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Failover and recovery semantics are stable enough for Hyperpush production incidents.
- Operators can inspect recovery state and failure causes with useful diagnostics.
- Soak or load evidence exists for clustered runtime behavior on the Hyperpush path.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/9 — Mesh: harden failover and recovery semantics for Hyperpush workloads
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/10 — Mesh: expose operator diagnostics and soak verification for clustered runtime behavior
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

requested.title_after: Mesh: deploy and release primitives needed for Hyperpush autonomous remediation
requested.body_after:
## Outcome
Provide the Mesh-side primitives needed for Hyperpush's autonomous remediation loop to deploy fixes, verify releases, and reason about runtime truth safely.

## Scope
- Release/deploy/runtime surfaces needed by Hyperpush's agent loop
- Post-deploy verification hooks or signals needed to confirm error-rate drops
- Support for truthful autonomous deploy + rollback/reopen behavior where appropriate
- Any missing runtime/build/deploy seams that block the GitHub-to-production loop

## Acceptance criteria
- Hyperpush can depend on Mesh/runtime surfaces for deploy + verify flows without hidden operator work
- Post-release verification is grounded in real runtime signals
- Missing primitives are added or the Hyperpush product claim is narrowed truthfully

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Mesh
- Track: Deployment
- Commitment: Planned
- Critical path: 1 -> 4

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Release, deploy, and runtime verification signals are available to the Hyperpush product.
- Post-release verification can rely on those real signals.
- Rollback or regression behavior is supported or explicitly bounded.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/11 — Mesh: expose release and deploy verification signals for autonomous remediation
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/12 — Mesh: add rollback and regression primitives for failed autonomous fixes

after.title: Mesh: deploy and release primitives needed for Hyperpush autonomous remediation
after.body:
## Outcome
Provide the Mesh-side primitives needed for Hyperpush's autonomous remediation loop to deploy fixes, verify releases, and reason about runtime truth safely.

## Scope
- Release/deploy/runtime surfaces needed by Hyperpush's agent loop
- Post-deploy verification hooks or signals needed to confirm error-rate drops
- Support for truthful autonomous deploy + rollback/reopen behavior where appropriate
- Any missing runtime/build/deploy seams that block the GitHub-to-production loop

## Acceptance criteria
- Hyperpush can depend on Mesh/runtime surfaces for deploy + verify flows without hidden operator work
- Post-release verification is grounded in real runtime signals
- Missing primitives are added or the Hyperpush product claim is narrowed truthfully

## Roadmap notes
- Domain: Mesh
- Track: Deployment
- Commitment: Planned
- Critical path: 1 -> 4

<!-- roadmap-detail-start -->
## Detailed acceptance criteria
- Release, deploy, and runtime verification signals are available to the Hyperpush product.
- Post-release verification can rely on those real signals.
- Rollback or regression behavior is supported or explicitly bounded.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/11 — Mesh: expose release and deploy verification signals for autonomous remediation
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/mesh-lang/issues/12 — Mesh: add rollback and regression primitives for failed autonomous fixes
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

requested.title_after: Hyperpush: promote frontend-exp into the real operator app while demoting landing to marketing
requested.body_after:
## Outcome
Promote `frontend-exp` into the real Hyperpush operator app and reduce `landing` to a pure marketing site.

## Current state
Partial only; real backend/data workflows for the operator app remain open. Public repo truth should read `hyperpush-org/hyperpush` even though local workspace evidence still references `mesher/frontend-exp/lib/mock-data.ts via the compatibility symlink into the sibling product repo`. This issue remains active follow-through because the current operator surfaces are still partially mock-backed rather than fully shipped.

## Scope
- Rename/reposition `frontend-exp` into the real app surface
- `landing` becomes marketing only
- Replace mocked dashboard surfaces with real operator data and workflows
- All major product screens in the current prototype become truthful: Issues, Performance, Solana Programs, Releases, Alerts, AI Copilot, Bounties, Dashboards, Treasury, Discover

## Acceptance criteria
- The operator app is the real product control plane
- The landing site no longer acts as a fake or partial app
- Core app navigation and page surfaces are backed by real data and workflows
- The wording stays aligned with the current repo/code truth instead of relying on stale cross-repo or local-path assumptions.
- The issue does not treat the current mock-backed operator surfaces as already shipped.

## Detailed acceptance criteria
- `frontend-exp` is promoted into the real operator app and `landing` is marketing only.
- The operator app's major screens are backed by real data and workflows.
- The public product surface no longer relies on mocked app behavior.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/33 — Hyperpush app: promote frontend-exp and retire landing-as-app behavior
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/34 — Hyperpush app: replace mocked operator screens with real data and workflows
<!-- roadmap-detail-end -->

## Tracker context
- Labels: enhancement, epic, roadmap
- Project-backed: yes

requested.title_after: Hyperpush launch roadmap (product-repo execution umbrella)
requested.body_after:
## Scope
This umbrella tracks the truthful product-repo launch work that still belongs in `hyperpush-org/hyperpush`. Cross-repo Mesh work is linked as an external dependency instead of being treated as product-repo scope.

## Current state
The original umbrella mixed product work, mesh-lang work, and stale repo-ownership assumptions. The reconciliation plan keeps the umbrella open, but narrows it to product-repo execution and explicit dependency links.

## Product epics
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#11 — Hyperpush: JS SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#12 — Hyperpush: Go SDK true drop-in Sentry compatibility
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#13 — Hyperpush: core parity for ingestion, grouping, regression, alerts, ownership, and environments
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#14 — Hyperpush: parity for releases, deploy correlation, symbolication, performance tracing, replay, retention, and sampling
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#15 — Hyperpush: promote frontend-exp into the real operator app while demoting landing to marketing
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#16 — Hyperpush: SaaS onboarding and control plane at app.hyperpush.dev
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#17 — Hyperpush: AI Copilot chat-first operator control for all non-destructive actions
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#18 — Hyperpush: GitHub autonomous remediation loop (issue → patch → PR → CI → merge)
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#19 — Hyperpush: auto-deploy, post-release verification, and automatic regression reopening
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#20 — Hyperpush: public bug board, contributor race model, maintainer-approved payouts, and X bot distribution
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#21 — Hyperpush: Solana treasury, configurable token/SOL payouts, and whale-proof token mechanics
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#22 — Hyperpush: self-hosted parity with SaaS for all core features using BYO infrastructure and secrets
- [ ] hyperpush-org/hyperpush#23 — Hyperpush: generic Docker-on-a-VM deployment baseline for frontend and meshable backend

## External dependencies
- hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#3 — Mesh clustered Docker-on-VM deploy baseline for Hyperpush
- hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#4 — Mesh runtime hardening for Hyperpush production load and clustering
- hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#5 — Mesh deploy and release primitives needed for Hyperpush autonomous remediation
- hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#6 — Mesh docs, packages, installers, and public surfaces must stay truthful for Hyperpush launch
- hyperpush-org/mesh-lang#15 — Mesh built-in runtime observability canvas for nodes, actors, and message flow

## Acceptance criteria
- The umbrella only tracks work that still belongs in `hyperpush-org/hyperpush`.
- Mesh-language/runtime/docs dependencies stay linked, but they are not flattened into product-repo scope.
- Public repo wording stays truthful even though the local workspace still carries the historical `hyperpush-mono` path alias.

requested.body_after:
## Parent epic
- hyperpush-org/hyperpush#15

## Outcome
Replace prototype/mocked screens with real product data and workflows across the operator app.

## Current state
Partial only; real backend/data workflows for the operator app remain open. Public repo truth should read `hyperpush-org/hyperpush` even though local workspace evidence still references `mesher/frontend-exp/lib/mock-data.ts via the compatibility symlink into the sibling product repo`. This issue remains active follow-through because the current operator surfaces are still partially mock-backed rather than fully shipped.

## Acceptance criteria
- Issues, performance, releases, bounties, treasury, and related screens are real
- Operators can complete meaningful workflows in-app
- UI no longer overclaims unsupported behavior
- The wording stays aligned with the current repo/code truth instead of relying on stale cross-repo or local-path assumptions.
- The issue does not treat the current mock-backed operator surfaces as already shipped.

## Detailed acceptance criteria
- The promoted operator app must stop depending on mock data for the backend surfaces Mesher already exposes today.
- Existing Mesher APIs (issues, dashboard, detail, alerts, settings, team, API keys, storage, and live updates) should be integrated before later-stage Solana/bounty/treasury/discover work is treated as active UI conversion.
- Auth/session/project-selection should be defined as a bridge from the current Mesher contract, not invented independently of it.

## Initial sub-issues
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/51 — Hyperpush app backend: define the bridge from the current Mesher contract to real app auth, session, and project selection
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/52 — Hyperpush app backend: replace issue, dashboard, detail, and alert mock data with existing Mesher APIs and live updates
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/53 — Hyperpush app backend: stage Solana, bounties, treasury, and discover integration behind real backing services
- [ ] https://github.com/hyperpush-org/hyperpush/issues/57 — Hyperpush app backend: wire project settings, team, API keys, and storage admin surfaces to existing Mesher APIs

## Tracker context
- Labels: enhancement, roadmap
- Project-backed: yes
